task_completion.description: Verify that the sales_data CSV file has been imported into Power BI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Data pane on the right side of the Power BI interface. A table named 'sales_data' should be visible.

task_completion.description: Verify that the report page is named correctly.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the bottom left of the report canvas. The active page tab should be named 'Pareto Analysis'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the required DAX measures have been created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the Data pane under the 'sales_data' table. There should be items named 'Rep_Revenue' and 'Cumulative_Pct', indicated by a calculator icon next to them.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Card visual showing Rep_Revenue is present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the report canvas for a Card visual (a simple box displaying a single large number). It should display the total Rep_Revenue, which should be approximately 5.02M.

task_completion.description: Verify that a Line and Clustered Column Chart is present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look at the report canvas for a combo chart containing both vertical columns (for revenue) and a line (for cumulative percentage) plotted against sales representatives on the X-axis.

task_completion.description: Verify that the combo chart correctly displays the Pareto analysis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Check the X-axis of the combo chart. The sales representatives must be ordered descending by revenue: David, Bob, Eve, Alice, Charlie. The line should start at the top of David's column (around 22.7%) and slope upwards to the right, ending at exactly 100% for Charlie.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the actual Power BI Desktop application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots should show the genuine Power BI Desktop interface, including the ribbon, canvas, Visualizations pane, and Data pane, rather than a text editor or image viewer.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not use terminal commands to bypass GUI interaction.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no PowerShell or command prompt windows open showing scripts being used to generate the .pbix file or manipulate the report directly.

integrity.description: Verify that the visuals on the canvas are genuine Power BI elements.
integrity.visual_evidence: The charts and cards should have standard Power BI UI elements, such as titles, axes, legends, and selection borders when active, rather than appearing as pasted static images.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The dataset contains 5 sales representatives. Their total revenues are: David ($1,141,737.36), Bob ($1,080,990.63), Eve ($970,183.99), Alice ($965,541.77), and Charlie ($860,811.48). The total revenue is approximately $5.02M. The combo chart must show the representatives on the X-axis in this exact descending order: David, Bob, Eve, Alice, Charlie. The line for Cumulative_Pct should start at approximately 22.7% for David and reach exactly 100% for Charlie. The Card visual should display a value around 5.02M.
